title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE CAA HIGH SCHOOL ANNEX IN BARANGAY BF INTERNATIONAL/CAA, LAS PI脙聭AS CITY FROM THE LAS PI脙聭AS NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS THE CAA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 5138 / 103 OG No. 37, 5824 (Sept. 20, 2007)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 9452, May 17, 2007 ]

AN ACT SEPARATING THE CAA HIGH SCHOOL ANNEX IN BARANGAY BF INTERNATIONAL/CAA, LAS PI脙聭AS CITY FROM THE LAS PI脙聭AS NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS THE CAA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representative of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Separation and Conversion into a National High School.

芒聙聰 The CAA High School Annex in Barangay BF International/CAA, Las Pi帽as City is hereby separated from the Las Pi帽as National High School and converted into an independent national high school to be known as the CAA National High School.

SEC. 2.

Transfer of Assets and Liabilities.

芒聙聰 All personnel, assets, liabilities and records of the CAA High School Annex are hereby transferred to and absorbed by the CAA National High School.

SEC. 3.

Appropriations.

芒聙聰 The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the Department's program the operationalization of the CAA National High School, the initial funding of which shall be charged against the current year's appropriations of the Las Pi帽as National High School 芒聙聰 CAA High School Annex. Thereafter, the amount necessary for the continued operation of the school shall be included in the annual General Approriations Act.

SEC. 4.

Implementation.

芒聙聰 The Secretary of Education shall issue such rules and regulations as may ne neccessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 5.

Effectivity.

芒聙聰 This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the Official Gazette.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JOSE DE VENECIA JR.

(Sgd.) MANNY VILLAR

Speaker of the House of Representatives

President of the Senate

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on April 5, 2006 and February 19, 2007, respectively.

(Sgd.) ROBERTO P. NAZARENO

(Sgd.) OSCAR G. YABES

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Secretary of the Senate

Approved: MAY 15 2007

(Sgd.) GLORIA MACAPAGAL - ARROYO

President of the Philippines
